colours.//: # Editor Colours
## Background Elements

The editor area background needs to be dark, but just light enough that it isn't difficult to phase out when reading code. All other background colours are derived from this. Desaturated version of `#001b1b` (cannot be darker), 1:1 with `#191919`.
`editorBg` 1:1 with `tabsBg`, used for the title bar when inactive and blank editor areas
`editorBg` 9:1 with `text` (cannot be brighter or darker), used to distinguish multiple editor areas
Scrollbar backgrounds, all white opacities were carefully chosen to show what is behind them while remaining visible
### Tabs
`editorBg` 4:1 with black (cannot be brighter), used as editor gutter (tab bar and line numbers background), also used by inactive tabs
`tabsBg` 15:1 with white (cannot be brighter or darker), used by current editor area's active tab
`editorBg` 3:2 with `tabsBg`, used by inactive tabs, visually halfway between the two
### Widgets
Shadow just dark enough to be distinguishable from editor areas and all text found within them, used for widgets and the top of the editor area when scrolled
`editorBg` 1:2 with `sidebarsBg`, used for single-line and multi-line code blocks
`editorBg` 1:2 with `tabsBg`, used for blockquotes (cannot be darker). Darker instead of brighter due to blockquotes tending to be tangential thoughts or side notes.
`editorBg` 15:1 with `text`, used as the left border for blockquotes (especially needed when nested)
## Text Elements

The editor area foreground, which all token colours operate on. White is the only sensible colour for base text. Saturated version of `#c4c4c4` (between `#c0c0c0` and `#c8c8c8`), 1:7 with `#00d0d0` (cyan barely noticeable).
`text` 5:7 with white (just bright enough to not look disabled), which looks good when sparingly used with important text and icons, so long as they are thin
`text` 11:2 with `editorBg`, makes cursor blend in with surrounding text
Cyan 1:3 with white, then 5:3 with `cursor`, only used when there are multiple cursors
`text` 14:15 with black, used for comments and ignored text
`comments` 2:5 with `editorBg`
Links ought to show off the theme accent colour, as not many components do. For orange, 4:1 with white (cannot be brighter).
Used specifically for links in the editor, e.g. Markdown links. For orange, 6:1 with white.
Active links are allowed to look washed-out, but must still be distinguishable. For orange, 1:5 with white.
Text selection backgrounds, all white opacities were carefully chosen to show what they are highlighting while remaining visible, even when stacked
Selection backgrounds not from the user should be darker, word highlights taking less priority
## Functional Rainbow
Keywords and control flow direct the execution of a program. They lie somewhere between functions (blue) and classes (red), being rigidly-defined functions themselves. `#ff006a` 11:16 with `#c7dbdb`.
`controlFlow` 1:1 with `functions`, used for icons that sit inbetween the two
Functions use blue to represent stability. `#2424db` 1:2 with `#dae7e7`.
Variables use yellow to represent creativity. `#ffff00` 1:2 with `#e5e5e5`.
`variables` 29:18 with `constantValues`
Constant values use green to represent growth. `#00ff00` 7:2 with `#486a6a`.
`constantValues` 3:2 with `inputBg`, used for sidebar icons that are too bright when set to `constantValues`

Types objectify variables, rigidly defining their scope and usage. They lie somewhere between variables (yellow) and classes (red), being rigidly-defined variables themselves. `#ff8000` 4:1 with `#00f5f5`.
Classes use red to represent strength. `#ff0000` 11:8 with `#ffffff`.
# Sidebar Colours
## Background Elements
`editorBg` 2:1 with black (cannot be brighter), used for the majority of sidebars that surround the editor (including the title bar)
Same as `tabsBgActiveFocused`, used only for tiny section headers and separate-purpose sidebars (e.g. sidebar sections and status bar)
Rather than using a single colour, the status bar will show off the accent colour while debugging. For orange, 5:6 with `sidebarsBg`.
Same process as `tabsBgActiveFocused`. For orange, 10:1 with white.
`text` 5:1 with `editorBg` (cannot be darker), used for icons in sidebars and text in title bar and status bar
Twitter-style verified checkmark for extensions
Used to highlight components with a thin border. For orange, 1:4 with `buttonsBg`.
## Input Elements
List item selection backgrounds, all white opacities were carefully chosen to work well on various backgrounds and keep what is in front of them visible
Large buttons background. For orange, 6:1 with `sidebarsBg`.
Large buttons background when hovered. For orange, 25:1 with white.
Small buttons inside inputs background, which are otherwise transparent. For orange, 8:21 with `inputsBg`.
## Errors & Warnings
Darker version of `classes`, `#ff0000` 5:2 with `#ffffff`, must remain legible on editor area background and current file selection
Darker version of `types`, `#ff8000` 6:1 with `#00f5f5`
`comments` 5:2 with `editorBg`, used to maximally reduce importance of info messages
# Extra Colours
## Activity Bar
`editorBg` 3:5 with black (cannot be brighter or darker)
Saturated version of `#6e6e6e` (cannot be darker), 3:1 with `#007a7a`, made to be bright enough to not look disabled
`activityBarIcons` 2:5 with `activityBarBg` (cannot be brighter or darker), used for notifications on the activity bar to reduce their importance
## Terminal
`editorBg` 2:7 with black (cannot be brighter or darker), the terminal is a special case as all sorts of colours can be displayed on it
